costEstimator.title: Home Maintenance Cost Estimator — Annual Budget Calculator
costEstimator.description: Estimate your annual home maintenance costs based on home specifications and location.
costEstimator.lowRangeDesc: Minimal maintenance year — just filter changes, minor repairs, and routine inspections.
costEstimator.midRangeDesc: Typical year — includes one or two professional services, some component replacements.
costEstimator.highRangeDesc: High-cost year — major repairs, system replacements, or unexpected failures.
costEstimator.disclaimerText: This is an educational estimate based on the industry-standard 1% rule and adjusted averages. Actual maintenance costs vary significantly based on local labor rates, material costs, home condition, and unforeseen issues. This is not financial or home warranty advice. Always budget for emergencies and consult a professional for specific cost assessments.
costEstimator.enterSize: Enter a home size above to see your estimate.

compare.affiliate_disclaimer: Shopping links are optional and may be affiliate links. Always verify product coverage, availability, delivery fees, and local code requirements before buying.
compare.concrete_sourcing_desc: Best for permanent patios. Order ready-mix delivery for projects over 1.5 cubic yards.
compare.paver_sourcing_desc: Perfect for modular patio setups. Order individually or by the pallet.
compare.gravel_sourcing_desc: Low-cost pathway option. Pick up bags locally or order bulk delivery.

common.divide_cu_ft_by_27: Divide cubic feet by 27 to find cubic yards
common.multiply_area_by_thickness: Multiply Area by converted Thickness, then apply the
common.diy_note_text: You save on contractor fees but need tools (wheelbarrow, floats, screed, shovel) and helper hands. 1 cubic yard requires ~45 bags of 80lb mix.
common.contractor_note_text: Includes subgrade preparation, setting wooden formwork, placing reinforcement mesh/rebar, pouring, and trowel finish.

ice_water_shield.eave_length_helper: Total linear ft of eaves + rakes
ice_water_shield.valley_width_helper: Width on each side (24 in = 2 ft)
ice_water_shield.install_note_1: Apply ice & water shield starting at the eave edge, extending at least 24 inches past the interior wall line (typically 3 ft from the eave edge in cold climates).
ice_water_shield.install_note_2: Valley applications should extend 12-24 inches on each side of the valley centerline. Overlap horizontal strips by 6 inches.

snow_load.exceeds_warning: Consider removing snow from the roof immediately. This load may exceed standard residential design capacity.

tile.box_coverage_hint: If the store lists box coverage in sq ft instead of tile count, divide by box coverage and round up.

calculator_hub.no_results: No calculators found matching your search.

house_explorer.drag_hint: Drag to rotate, click to select a part, click again to open — or use arrow keys to navigate.

house_explorer.aria_map: Interactive house map showing roofing, walls, interior paint, foundation slabs, and driveway gardens. Use arrow keys to select layers, space/enter to view.
house_explorer.aria_svg: Interactive house map showing roofing, walls, interior paint, foundation slabs, and driveway gardens. Drag to rotate structure.
house_explorer.aria_exterior: Yard and patio layer. Click to explore landscaping, gravel, sand, and base material tools.
house_explorer.aria_foundation: Foundation and concrete slab layer. Click to explore concrete, footings, and masonry calculator.
house_explorer.aria_interior: Interior paint and tile flooring layer. Click to explore interior paint, floor tiles, and tiling grids.
house_explorer.aria_walls: Exterior wall and drywall structure layer. Click to explore drywall sheets, studs, framing, and masonry blocks.
house_explorer.aria_roof: Roofing layer. Click to explore roof shingles, metal roof, r-value insulation, and deck plans.

budget.no_plans_desc: Create a renovation budget plan to track your project costs. Add your own material and labor prices to stay on budget.
budget.plan_created_desc: {name} is ready. Add line items to start tracking costs.
budget.create_info: You will add material costs, labor, and other line items after creating the plan. Your budget data is stored locally in your browser.
budget.plan_not_found_desc: This budget plan could not be found or the URL is invalid.
budget.no_items: No line items yet. Add materials, labor, and other costs to build your budget.

summary.concrete_slab: You are planning a concrete slab project measuring {length} {unit} in length by {width} {unit} in width, with a thickness of {thickness} inches. This covers a total surface area of {area} {areaUnit}.
summary.drywall: You are planning a drywall installation with a total wall length of {wallLengths} ft and wall height of {wallHeight} ft, covering a total surface wall area of {wallArea} sq ft.

breakdown.intro: Based on your input parameters, the following exact quantities are required for the installation:

waste.high: A high waste allowance of {waste}% was included. This is highly recommended for projects with complex geometries, circular cutouts, irregular corners, or uneven subgrade levels to ensure you do not run short.
waste.low: A very tight waste margin of {waste}% has been specified. Ensure that your formwork is highly accurate and there is minimal spillage during placement, as this leaves very little margin for error.
waste.normal: A standard waste allowance of {waste}% has been factored in to compensate for cutting losses, spillage, and variations in subgrade levels or framing irregularities.

delivery.truck: Your project requires {volume} cubic yards of concrete ({bags} bags of {bagSize}). Because this volume is substantial, we strongly recommend purchasing ready-mix delivery instead of bags, which will save significant manual labor and ensure a uniform pour.
delivery.heavy: The total material load exceeds 3,000 lbs. Standard consumer pickup trucks can typically carry between 1,500 and 2,500 lbs in the bed. To prevent vehicle damage or unsafe transport, we recommend arranging professional home delivery from your retailer.
delivery.pickup: The total material load is approximately {weight} lbs, which can be safely transported in a standard light-duty pickup truck or cargo van in a single trip.

weight.desc: The total estimated weight of the required physical materials is {lbs} lbs (approx. {tons} tons). Make sure your workspace floor or subgrade can support this load and that you have helper labor available to lift and stack materials safely.

mixing.concrete_heavy: Mixing {bags} bags of concrete by hand is extremely taxing. We recommend renting a motorized drum mixer from a local tool rental center or opting for a ready-mix delivery truck to complete the project in one cohesive pour.
mixing.concrete_medium: With {bags} bags required, you can mix this by hand using a wheelbarrow and concrete hoe, though a rented electric mixer will make the job substantially faster.
mixing.concrete_light: This small quantity ({bags} bags) can be easily mixed by hand in a standard wheelbarrow, mortar tub, or five-gallon bucket.
mixing.fallback: Standard mixing or application steps are recommended. Follow packaging instructions.
